comment.body: Fairly new sub here, thanks so much for the awesome report (and other materials)! Still trying to learn as much as possible. May I make a suggestion? For some charts both x and y axis are missing and imho it’d be clearer to show them, e.g. yields or 30y Japan Bonds and similarly others. It’s unclear which time scale, granularity the chart corresponds to unless the readers follow those closely.

comment.body:
I have a bit of a naive question. How do you usually confirm a breakout?
Today the 6907 level felt like the hardest trade of the week — both going long and going short felt very uncomfortable. How do you usually enter around pivots like this? I’ve reviewed Alma’s previous trading strategies multiple times, but I still don’t fully understand it.

A breakout confirmation for me looks like this: the volume of the candle is at least 28% higher than the rolling 5-period mean, its body is 68% larger than the rolling mean, then it retests the pivot, calms down, and builds a structure beyond that pivot.
From an Elliott-wave perspective, for example, it will draw either a 3-wave pattern (fakeout) or a 5-wave pattern (breakout).
comment.raw_body:

A breakout confirmation for me looks like this: the volume of the candle is at least 28% higher than the rolling 5-period mean, its body is 68% larger than the rolling mean, then it retests the pivot, calms down, and builds a structure beyond that pivot.
From an Elliott-wave perspective, for example, it will draw either a 3-wave pattern (fakeout) or a 5-wave pattern (breakout).
